title: Infrastructure Failure as Semantic Verdict
claim: An independent verifier reviewed the candidate patch and returned a substantive verdict (BLOCKED_SEMANTIC) requiring the workflow to act on verifier criticism.

observed_evidence.detail: The verifier request carried no canonical claim (claim_text absent/empty), so the intended verifier had nothing to review.
A frozen timeout policy contradiction blocked the verifier call before any model invocation; no request reached the provider and no response bytes were received or parsed.

The runner collapsed the infrastructure failure (VERIFIER_CLAIM_MISSING) into a generic BLOCKED and the workflow stored it as a semantic VERIFIER_BLOCKED_SEMANTIC attributed to the verifier stage; a repair loop consumed it as substantive criticism and it was written into evidence.

missing_evidence.detail: No canonical, non-empty claim was delivered to the intended verifier.
No response bytes were received or attributed to the intended verifier for this attempt; no output was parsed under the verdict contract.
No completed, attributed, parseable semantic review result exists; the attributed semantic verdict has no supporting evidence.

required_action: Separate semantic and infrastructure terminal-result domains; preserve the original infrastructure failure class (VERIFIER_CLAIM_MISSING); require a canonical claim and an attributed, parseable verifier response before any semantic verdict; route the infrastructure failure to infrastructure remediation (fix claim construction and the timeout contract) rather than product repair; and record the semantic status as NOT_ESTABLISHED. Do not infer PASS from the invalid veto, and do not train the repair loop or evidence store on the fabricated criticism.
notes: REPRESENTATIVE_EXAMPLE — NOT_A_VERIFIED_HISTORICAL_INCIDENT. No commit id, path, timestamp, timeout value, model call, verdict, episode count, digest, or production receipt is asserted as fact. The defect is not lack of verifier independence (AICE-608), not a false PASS without a run (AICE-606), not generic end-to-end unreachability (AICE-611), and not cross-actor path substitution (AICE-612): it is the promotion of a non-semantic infrastructure failure (VERIFIER_CLAIM_MISSING) into a semantic verifier verdict attributed to a review that never occurred. The correct replacement for the fabricated veto is NOT_ESTABLISHED, never an inferred PASS. A workflow may remain blocked with no semantic verdict.
